section.heading: Roles and responsibilities of the Office of Management and Budget

paragraph.chapeau: in consultation with the Administrator and the Secretary, issue guidance that—

subparagraph.content: specifies the categories or characteristics of cloud computing products and services that are within the scope of FedRAMP;
includes requirements for agencies to obtain a FedRAMP authorization when operating a cloud computing product or service described in subparagraph (A) as a Federal information system; and
encompasses, to the greatest extent practicable, all necessary and appropriate cloud computing products and services;

paragraph.content: issue guidance describing additional responsibilities of FedRAMP and the FedRAMP Board to accelerate the adoption of secure cloud computing products and services by the Federal Government;
in consultation with the Administrator, establish a process to periodically review FedRAMP authorization packages to support the secure authorization and reuse of secure cloud products and services;
oversee the effectiveness of FedRAMP and the FedRAMP Board, including the compliance by the FedRAMP Board with the duties described in section 3610(d); and
to the greatest extent practicable, encourage and promote consistency of the assessment, authorization, adoption, and use of secure cloud computing products and services within and across agencies.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 117–263, div. E, title LIX, § 5921(b), Dec. 23, 2022, 136 Stat. 3454.)

note.heading: Repeal of Section
note.p: For repeal of section by section 5921(d)(1) of Pub. L. 117–263, see Effective Date of Repeal note below.

note.heading: Effective Date of Repeal
note.p: Pub. L. 117–263, div. E, title LIX, § 5921(d)(1), Dec. 23, 2022, 136 Stat. 3458, provided that the repeal of this section is effective on the date that is 5 years after Dec. 23, 2022.

note.heading: Construction
note.p: For rule of construction regarding section 5921 of Pub. L. 117–263, see section 5921(e) of Pub. L. 117–263, set out as a note under section 3607 of this title.
